q: Which two mechanisms extend the USER EXPERIENCE of Power Apps?
o: Power Apps component framework (PCF) and client scripting
why: PCF and client scripting extend the UX; plug-ins/Azure services extend server/integration.

q: Client scripting extends which app type's forms with event-driven logic?
why: Client scripts (JavaScript) run on model-driven form events via the Client API.

q: Decide whether each extensibility statement is true.

rows.s: PCF components can be reused across multiple apps.
Client script reacts to form and field events.
UX extensibility is the right place for heavy server transactions.

why: PCF is reusable UI; client script handles form events; heavy server logic belongs in plug-ins.

q: Which describes when UX extensibility is the wrong tool?
o: When logic must run reliably server-side regardless of the client
When you need a custom control
When you react to a field change
When you validate on the form
why: Client-side logic can be bypassed; server-side rules (plug-ins) enforce reliably.

q: Which two APIs let external/server code work with Dataverse?
o: The Web API and the Organization Service
The Graph API and OneDrive API
The Bing API and Maps API
The Excel API and Word API
why: Dataverse exposes the OData Web API and the .NET Organization Service.

q: Where do plug-ins execute within Dataverse?
o: On the event (execution) pipeline stages for a message
why: Plug-ins register on pipeline stages (pre-validation, pre/post-operation) for messages like Create/Update.

q: Decide whether each Dataverse extensibility statement is true.

rows.s: The Organization Service is a .NET SDK for Dataverse operations.
The Web API is a RESTful OData v4 endpoint.

why: Org Service (.NET) and Web API (OData) both access Dataverse; plug-ins run sync or async.

q: The event pipeline stage that runs BEFORE the main operation and can validate/cancel is...
why: Pre-validation and pre-operation run before the database operation; post-operation runs after.

q: What is a Custom API in Dataverse?
o: A reusable, definable operation (message) callable by clients and code
why: A Custom API defines a new bound/unbound Dataverse message with typed input/output parameters.

q: A Custom API can be invoked from...
o: Power Automate, the Web API, and plug-in/code callers
why: Once defined, a Custom API is a first-class message callable from flows, Web API and code.

q: Decide whether each Custom API statement is true.

rows.s: A Custom API can be bound to a table or unbound (global).
A Custom API can define input and output parameters.

why: Custom APIs are bound or unbound and declare typed request/response parameters.

q: Which pairing correctly matches a UX vs a server extension?
o: Custom API = UX; client script = server
Power BI = UX; Web API = client
why: Client script/PCF extend UX; plug-ins/Custom APIs extend server logic.

q: For simple declarative field logic (e.g., set/lock a field), which should you try FIRST?
why: Prefer business rules for simple declarative logic before writing client script or plug-ins.

q: Logic must run reliably on the server whenever a record is created, even via the API. Choose:
why: Only server-side code (a plug-in) runs regardless of the client, including API writes.

q: Decide whether each configure-vs-code guideline is true.

rows.s: Prefer flows/business rules over plug-ins when they meet the need.
Client script can be bypassed by non-form data entry paths.
You should always write a plug-in even for trivial UI logic.

why: Favor low-code where sufficient; remember client script isn't enforced server-side; don't over-code trivial UI logic.

q: A calculation must combine data and run for both UI and background updates. Best fit?
o: A plug-in (server-side) so it applies to every write path
why: Server-side plug-in logic applies uniformly across UI and background/API operations.
